what_it_does: This file is a roadmap and design charter for the Claude Code Extended Interconnection project: it lists features already built and proven, work in progress, planned next steps, and locked design laws.
observations: Built features are dated 2026-06-14 and include channel injection, cc_channels core, reply loop, group broadcast, MCP surface, @xsession GPU/loadout, and two-session live collaboration.
In-progress items are clone-to-channel and role skills.

Next items include capturing each session's claude session_id, an auto-join SessionStart hook, wiring group to persistent fabric channels, streaming voice from text deltas, reply aggregation in UI/RHM, sender gating, and semantic session find.
Auto-join is explicitly BLOCKED because editing ~/.claude.json was auto-denied as self-modification and needs Tim's approval.

Design laws define identity as working directory plus self-announced description, require replies to route through the mailbox and be pushed to the recipient, define group as the fabric channels concept, and treat the GPU loadout as a registry concern.
standouts: Channel injection into a live interactive session is called the hallmark of the system.
The reply loop pushes replies back into the asker's conversation without polling.
Two-session live collaboration uses a lead plus a time-travelled fork coordinated over the channel.
Clone-to-channel combines point-in-time materialization with an interactive channel launch.
Voice streaming depends on tapping text_delta from the supervisor _reader, which currently discards deltas.
Design laws are locked by Tim on 2026-06-14.
conventions: Uses emoji status headings: built/proven, in progress, next.
Records blockers and required owner approvals explicitly.
Treats identity as cwd + self-announced description rather than relying on Claude's session_id.
Abstracts engines/loadouts through a registry (@xsession) rather than direct configuration.
Distinguishes ad-hoc handle lists from the fabric channels/channel_act concept.
concerns: The roadmap mixes shipped, in-progress, blocked, and speculative items without clear priority or versioning.
Several next items depend on unspecified implementation details or external approvals.
Some terms (R3.4, RHM, embed-pplx, channel_act, supervisor _reader) are used without in-file definitions.
The date 2026-06-14 is in the future relative to typical present knowledge, suggesting it may be a project-specific reference or placeholder.
notes: A dated roadmap and locked design-law charter for a live-session interconnection fabric, showing what has shipped, what is blocked, and what is planned next.
questions: What is R3.4 and what does session_pointintime.materialize_at_point produce?
What is RHM in the context of reply aggregation UI?
What is the exact runtime/host for the @xsession GPU/loadout?
How would the SessionStart hook modify ~/.claude.json, and what policy governs the self-modification denial?
What is the difference between cc_channels and the fabric channels/channel_act concept?
What is embed-pplx and how is the transcript index built?
What is the supervisor _reader and how are text_delta messages currently discarded?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is titled and structured as a roadmap; it also serves as a locked design-law reference, so its purpose matches with an additional governance/constraint role.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to turn a collection of Claude Code sessions into an interconnected fabric where sessions can find, message, broadcast, clone, and speak to each other, while enforcing a small set of locked design rules about identity, routing, and engine management.
novelty: Channel injection into a live interactive session; a reply loop that pushes responses into the asker's conversation without polling; collaboration between a lead session and a time-travelled fork; per-session voice generation routed through a fabric engine; semantic session search over transcript embeddings.
contribution: It contributes a dated, structured feature trajectory and a set of locked design constraints that a fused system would keep as the authoritative plan and governance baseline for the interconnection fabric.
summary_for_embedding: A roadmap markdown file documenting completed, in-progress, and planned capabilities for a live Claude Code session interconnection fabric, including channel registry, reply routing, voice synthesis, clone-to-channel, and locked design laws.
intention_for_embedding: To chart the evolution and enforce the design constraints of a multi-session collaboration fabric for Claude Code.
